data.body:
Brainstorm design-of-record: OPTIONAL AUTHENTICATION FOR THE REBAR MCP SERVER (OAuth 2.1 Resource-Server model). Verified locally; AWS deploy + Gerrit/CI wiring are explicitly follow-on (Gerrit/CI do NOT consume MCP today — they use rebar as an in-process library/CLI per ADR-0007, so there is nothing to reconfigure there).

INVESTIGATION FINDINGS (grounded):
- rebar-mcp is STDIO-ONLY today (mcp.server.fastmcp.FastMCP("rebar") in src/rebar/mcp_server.py; build_server().run() with no transport arg). No HTTP transport, no network bind, no per-request/session/header plumbing. 48 tools via @mcp.tool(annotations=...). Config via McpConfig (src/rebar/_config_schema.py) + MCP_ENV_VARS (mcp_server.py) + server.json (CI drift gate scripts/check_server_manifest.py). Existing gates readonly/allow_llm/allow_jira are the reuse pattern.
- Nobody consumes MCP remotely: review_bot/app.py is a FastAPI webhook receiver calling the rebar LIBRARY in-process (ADR-0007, deliberately NOT MCP-over-HTTP); all .github/workflows use rebar CLI/library; the in-flight op-cert service (ee0b) is ANOTHER separate FastAPI library-mode service, explicitly NOT rebar-mcp.
- MCP spec: auth is OPTIONAL and HTTP-transport-only; stdio SHOULD NOT authenticate (creds from env). Server = OAuth 2.1 Resource Server: validate bearer tokens, advertise RFC 9728 Protected Resource Metadata, 401 + WWW-Authenticate. Server MUST validate token audience (RFC 8707); MUST NOT pass client tokens upstream (confused deputy).

DECISIONS (user-confirmed):
1. Scope = optional auth for MCP + verify LOCALLY. AWS/Gerrit/CI = follow-on.
2. BUILD OUR OWN verifiers on the official SDK's TokenVerifier seam (keep mcp SDK; pin mcp>=1.28,<2 — v2-alpha renames FastMCP->MCPServer). Resource-Server ONLY; NO Authorization Server / no DCR (delegate identity to the deploying org's IdP). Rejected adopting standalone fastmcp: heavy dep (~15 extra libs incl the whole mcp SDK), and fastmcp.server.auth is semver-EXEMPT (may break on patch; had CVEs) — the community mitigates via exact-pin + resource-server-posture + managed IdP, which is exactly what building-our-own gives us for ~50-100 LOC.
3. Ship the FULL ecosystem set of verifiers behind one composite seam: static bearer, JWKS/JWT (OIDC), RFC 7662 introspection, trusted-proxy passthrough, pluggable custom. PAT(static)+OAuth(JWKS/JWT) are the FLOOR (must-have); introspection/proxy/custom are lower priority within the epic.
4. GitHub-USER auth and Gerrit are OUT OF SCOPE (neither is an auth provider; GitHub user tokens are opaque with no JWKS/RFC7662; Gerrit is not a token issuer — both are only ever a deploying org's proxy/custom concern). Coverage: JWKS covers Cognito/Entra/Auth0/Okta/Keycloak/WorkOS/Google-ID/GitHub-Actions-OIDC natively; introspection covers standards-compliant opaque tokens; trusted-proxy+custom are the catch-all.

SECURITY POSTURE (baked in; reviewer-facing): every verifier validates aud/resource itself (SDK does NOT — equivalent to always-on --oauth-strict); fail-closed everywhere; HTTP with auth disabled REFUSES TO START unless REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_UNAUTHENTICATED_HTTP=1; DNS-rebinding/Origin protection ON by default (TransportSecuritySettings), loopback bind default, explicit allowlist for real hosts; JWT algorithm pinning (reject alg=none / RS-HS confusion), SSRF-safe JWKS fetch + rotation cache; static = constant-time compare + hash-at-rest; introspection fail-closed + short-TTL positive-only cache; trusted-proxy = loopback + shared-secret + strip client identity headers; never log tokens; TLS assumed terminated at edge; no token passthrough upstream. CVE context: FastMCP CVE-2026-27124 (OAuthProxy confused-deputy), GHSA-vv7q-7jx5-f767 (OpenAPI SSRF), SDK GHSA-9h52-p55h-vw2f (DNS-rebinding default-off), oauth2-proxy CVE-2026-40575 (X-Forwarded header spoofing).

SDK API (mcp v1.28.1): TokenVerifier.verify_token(token)->AccessToken|None; AccessToken(token,client_id,scopes,expires_at,resource,subject,claims); AuthSettings(issuer_url,resource_server_url,required_scopes); FastMCP(token_verifier=,auth=,host=,port=,transport_security=); run(transport="streamable-http"); TransportSecuritySettings(enable_dns_rebinding_protection,allowed_hosts,allowed_origins); PRM served at /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource; SDK does NOT validate aud (verifier must). Test via httpx ASGITransport against mcp.streamable_http_app() under mcp.session_manager.run().

DECOMPOSITION: S1 HTTP transport + transport hardening (foundation); S2 auth framework + TokenVerifier/AuthSettings/PRM wiring + static verifier (admission core + floor pt1); S3 JWKS/JWT verifier (floor pt2, adds pyjwt[crypto]); S4 RFC7662 introspection; S5 trusted-proxy verifier+middleware; S6 pluggable custom verifier; S7 docs (docs/mcp-auth.md) + ADR + regenerated mcp-reference/env-vars/server.json. Deps: S2<-S1; S3/S4/S5/S6<-S2; S7<-all. New module src/rebar/_mcp_auth.py.
